programming: On-board: requires 2 working programmed keys; cycle ignition off-on within 10 s to program new key. All-keys-lost requires scan tool.
notes: B111-PT uses Z/B106 keyway. Circle+ chip (non-rewritable ID46). 2007-2013 range confirmed across multiple sources. Immobilizer is Pass-Key III+ (transponder-based), not PassLock (which is resistor-based with no transponder).

programming: Remote: no on-board self-program; requires scan tool or dealer. Transponder standalone: on-board possible with 2 existing programmed keys.
notes: 315 MHz remote FCC M3N-32337100 covers Silverado/Sierra 2014-2018 and Colorado/Canyon 2015-2021. HU100 keyway replaces older Z/B106 on new-body trucks.

programming: Smart key: no on-board programming; scan tool required. Transponder: on-board with 2 existing keys or scan tool.
notes: 2019-2020 uses HYQ1EA at 433.92 MHz (FCC-confirmed; some aftermarket sources incorrectly list 434 MHz). Push-start trims receive Smart key; standard-ignition trims may still use B119 transponder.

programming: Scan tool required; no on-board self-program. Professional locksmith or dealer only.
notes: HYQ1ES at 433.92 MHz (FCC-confirmed; some aftermarket listings erroneously state 434 MHz). 5-button with tailgate function. Upgrade from HYQ1EA generation. yearEnd corrected to 2021 — 2022+ mid-year shifted to YG0G21TB2/ID49. TRIM-DEPENDENT: push-button-start trims (LT, LTZ, RST, High Country, Trail Boss) use Smart/HYQ1ES; WT, Custom, and Custom Trail Boss use traditional key ignition (B119-PT transponder, no push button start). Starting with 2022 refresh, push-button start became standard on all 1500 trims.

programming: Scan tool required; VIP vehicles have no low-speed CAN circuit — AUTEL IM608 Pro or equivalent needed.
notes: 2022 changed mid-year to VIP architecture with ID49 HITAG PRO chip. Same FCC ID YG0G21TB2 shared with Tahoe/Suburban/Yukon 2021-2024 and Sierra 2022+. Frequency 433 MHz confirmed by multiple OEM listings and aftermarket sources.

programming: No on-board for remote; scan tool required. Transponder on-board with 2 existing keys.
notes: HD trucks share same key system as 1500 in this generation.

programming: Transponder: on-board with 2 existing keys. All-keys-lost: scan tool required.
notes: Silverado 2500/3500 HD 2019-2023: push-button / keyless start was NOT available on any HD trim — all trims used key-start ignition. Use B119-PT transponder only. Do NOT order HYQ1EA/HYQ1ES smart key fobs for these years — those FCCs are for 1500 trucks. 2024+ standard push-button — see next entry.

programming: Scan tool required; professional only. VIP architecture — AUTEL IM608 Pro or equivalent.
notes: 2024 Silverado HD redesign brought standard push-button start across all trims. YG0G21TB2 at 433 MHz, HITAG PRO ID49 chip. Same FCC as Sierra HD 2024+ and Silverado 1500 2022+.

programming: Remote: scan tool required. Transponder: on-board with 2 existing keys.
notes: 2015-2022 Colorado shares FCC M3N-32337100 at 315 MHz with Silverado 2014-2018 and Canyon 2015-2021.

programming: Scan tool required; no on-board programming.
notes: New-gen Colorado (2023+) moved to ID49/YG0G21TB2 platform at 433 MHz, consistent with 2023+ Canyon. Part number 13548436 confirmed by multiple OEM sources.

programming: On-board with 2 working keys; all-keys-lost needs scan tool.
notes: B111-PT Z-keyway covers Tahoe through 2014. Immobilizer is Pass-Key III+ (transponder-based), not PassLock.

variants.transponderChip: Philips ID46 GM EXT PCF7937E / NCF2951E

programming: Scan tool required; some models allow on-board with 2 existing keys via DIC menu.
notes: 315 MHz HYQ1AA generation (FCC confirms 314.9 MHz, rounded to 315). Shares key with GMC Yukon 2015-2020. HU100 emergency blade included.

programming: Scan tool required; no on-board programming.
notes: Upgraded to ID49/433 MHz with 2021 full redesign. Shares YG0G21TB2 with Suburban 2021-2024.

variants.transponderChip: Philips ID46 GM EXT PCF7937E / NCF2951E

programming: Scan tool required; on-board possible with 2 existing keys via DIC menu.
notes: Identical key system to Tahoe 2015-2020. HYQ1AA at 315 MHz.

programming: Scan tool required; no on-board programming.
notes: Same generation shift as Tahoe 2021+. YG0G21TB2 at 433 MHz.

programming: On-board with 2 working keys; all-keys-lost needs scan tool.
notes: B111-PT Z-keyway. Compatible through 2017 Traverse. Immobilizer is Pass-Key III+ (transponder-based).

programming: Scan tool required; no on-board programming.
notes: New-gen Traverse (2018-2019) uses HYQ4EA at 433.92 MHz (FCC-confirmed). Shared with Blazer 2019-2020. Some early 2020 production may still carry HYQ4EA — check door sticker. 2020+ switched to HYQ4ES.

variants.transponderChip: Philips ID46 GM EXT / Hitag 3 ID47

programming: DIY on-board possible with 2 existing keys; all-keys-lost requires scan tool.
notes: HYQ4ES at 434 MHz (433.92 MHz per FCC filing). Shared platform with Blazer and Trailblazer 2021+.

programming: Scan tool required; no on-board programming.
notes: First-gen Blazer shares HYQ4EA at 433 MHz with Traverse 2018-2020.

variants.transponderChip: Philips ID46 GM EXT / Hitag 3 ID47

programming: DIY on-board possible with 2 existing keys; all-keys-lost requires scan tool.
notes: HYQ4ES at 434 MHz (433.92 MHz per FCC filing). Shared platform with Traverse 2020+ and Trailblazer.

programming: On-board with 2 existing keys; all-keys-lost needs scan tool.
notes: B111-PT Z-keyway covering Sierra through early 2014. Immobilizer is Pass-Key III+ (transponder-based).

programming: Remote: scan tool required. Transponder: on-board with 2 existing keys.
notes: Shares identical system with Silverado 1500 2014-2018. M3N-32337100 at 315 MHz.

programming: Smart key: scan tool required. Transponder: on-board with 2 existing keys.
notes: Same FCC HYQ1EA as Silverado 2019-2020. FCC confirms 433.92 MHz. Some aftermarket sources erroneously list 434 MHz for truck applications.

programming: Scan tool required; no on-board programming.
notes: Upgraded to HYQ1ES/433 MHz for 2021 (FCC-confirmed; some aftermarket lists 434 MHz in error). TRIM-DEPENDENT: push-button-start trims (SLT, AT4, Denali) use Smart/HYQ1ES; base Regular and SLE trims with traditional key ignition use B119-PT transponder. Starting with 2022, push-button start became available across all Sierra 1500 trims.

programming: Scan tool required; VIP architecture — AUTEL IM608 Pro or equivalent.
notes: Shared YG0G21TB2 at 433 MHz with 2023-2024 Canyon. ID49 generation.

programming: Remote: scan tool required. Transponder: on-board with 2 existing keys.
notes: Sierra HD 2014-2018 shares key system with Silverado 1500 / Sierra 1500 of this era. M3N-32337100 RemoteHead (315 MHz) on higher trims; base trims transponder-only (B119-PT). 2019+ changed — see next entry.

programming: Transponder: on-board with 2 existing keys. All-keys-lost: scan tool required.
notes: Sierra HD 2019-2023: push-button / keyless start was NOT available on any HD trim — all trims used key-start ignition. Use B119-PT transponder only. Do NOT order a smart key fob for these years. 2024+ standard push-button — see next entry.

programming: Scan tool required; professional only. VIP architecture — AUTEL IM608 Pro or equivalent.
notes: 2024 Sierra HD redesign brought standard push-button start across all trims. YG0G21TB2 at 433 MHz, HITAG PRO ID49 chip. Same FCC as Canyon 2023+ and Silverado 1500 2022+.

programming: Remote: scan tool required. Transponder: on-board with 2 existing keys.
notes: Same key system as Colorado 2015-2022. M3N-32337100 at 315 MHz.

programming: Scan tool required; AUTEL IM608 Pro or equivalent for VIP architecture.
notes: New-gen Canyon fully redesigned for 2023; shares YG0G21TB2 at 433 MHz with Sierra 2022+.

programming: On-board with 2 existing keys; all-keys-lost needs scan tool.
notes: B111 Z-keyway generation for Yukon. Immobilizer is Pass-Key III+ (transponder-based).

variants.transponderChip: Philips ID46 GM EXT PCF7937E / NCF2951E

programming: Scan tool required; some models allow on-board with 2 existing keys.
notes: 315 MHz HYQ1AA generation. Shares key with Tahoe/Suburban 2015-2020.

programming: Scan tool required; no on-board programming.
notes: Same ID49/433 MHz platform as Silverado/Sierra/Tahoe/Suburban 2021+.

programming: On-board with 2 existing keys; all-keys-lost needs scan tool.
notes: First-gen Acadia uses B111-PT Z-keyway through 2016. Redesigned 2017 Acadia switched to HU100/smart-key platform. Immobilizer is Pass-Key III+ (transponder-based).

programming: Scan tool required; no on-board self-program.
notes: TRIM-DEPENDENT (2017-2019): PEPS trims use Smart/HYQ1EA proximity key (433 MHz); non-PEPS base trims (SL/SLE without keyless package) use OHT01060512 remote-head flip key (315 MHz). OHT01060512 compatibility confirmed for 2017-2019 only — 2020 Acadia is smart-key only across all trims. Redesigned 2017 Acadia uses HYQ1EA at 433 MHz (FCC-confirmed 433.92 MHz). Without hands-free trim uses 3-button variant.

programming: Scan tool required; no on-board programming.
notes: Upgraded to HYQ1ES for 2021+. FCC confirms 433.92 MHz. Same part as Terrain 2022-2024. Some aftermarket listings erroneously state 434 MHz.

programming: On-board with 2 existing keys; all-keys-lost needs scan tool.
notes: Terrain 2010-2017 uses B119-PT HU100 transponder. The OHT01060512 remote-head flip key (integrating transponder + remote in one unit) was the standard OEM factory-issued key across all 2010-2017 trims; a standalone B119-PT transponder blade can also be programmed as an alternative. No push-button start (PEPS) on any 2010-2017 Terrain trim.

programming: Scan tool required; no on-board programming.
notes: Redesigned 2018 Terrain uses HYQ1EA at 433 MHz (FCC-confirmed 433.92 MHz). Shares FCC with Acadia 2017-2020. TRIM-DEPENDENT: PEPS (keyless-entry) trims use Smart/HYQ1EA proximity key; non-PEPS base trims continue using the OHT01060512 remote-head flip key (315 MHz, same as 2010-2017 generation). The OHT01060512 flip key compatibility extends to 2022 on some Terrain trims.

programming: Scan tool required; no on-board programming.
notes: Upgraded to HYQ1ES for 2022+. FCC confirms 433.92 MHz. Shared with Acadia 2021-2024. Some aftermarket listings erroneously state 434 MHz.

programming: On-board with 2 existing keys; all-keys-lost needs scan tool.
notes: B111-PT Z-keyway for Escalade. yearStart corrected to 2007 — Escalade did not use a transponder key until 2007 (pre-2007 used PassLock without transponder). Immobilizer is Pass-Key III+.

variants.transponderChip: Philips ID46 GM EXT PCF7937E / NCF2951E

programming: Scan tool required; no on-board programming.
notes: Two FCC variants: HYQ2AB (315 MHz) is most common; HYQ2EB (433 MHz, FCC-confirmed 433.92 MHz) exists for some 2017+ trims (RPO code XL8). Verify via door sticker RPO codes XL7=315 MHz, XL8=433 MHz before ordering. HYQ2EB part number corrected to 13598512.

programming: Scan tool required; no on-board programming.
notes: Redesigned 2021 Escalade uses YG0G20TB1 (distinct from YG0G21TB2 on trucks). ID49 HITAG PRO chip. 6-button with hatch glass. TWO frequency variants: PN 13538866 = 315 MHz; PN 13538864/13541571 = 433 MHz. Always match original remote frequency before ordering.

programming: Scan tool required; professional locksmith or dealer.
notes: XT5 launch uses HYQ2EB at 433 MHz (FCC-confirmed 433.92 MHz; prior entry incorrectly stated 434 MHz). Also covers XT6 2020 and XT4 2019.

programming: Scan tool required; professional locksmith or dealer.
notes: HYQ2ES at 433 MHz (FCC-confirmed 433.92 MHz). Covers XT4/XT5/XT6 2020+. ILCO IAX00020100 (PRX-CAD-5B10).

programming: Scan tool required; professional locksmith or dealer.
notes: XT4 launched 2019 using HYQ2EB at 433 MHz (FCC-confirmed 433.92 MHz; prior entry incorrectly stated 434 MHz). Same as XT5 2017-2019.

programming: Scan tool required; professional locksmith or dealer.
notes: Upgraded to HYQ2ES for 2020+. FCC-confirmed 433.92 MHz. Shared with XT5/XT6 2020+.

programming: Scan tool required; professional locksmith or dealer.
notes: XT6 first model year 2020. Most early 2020 units use HYQ2EB at 433 MHz. Some 2020 production may already use HYQ2ES — verify FCC ID on door sticker. 2021+ standardized on HYQ2ES — see next entry.

programming: Scan tool required; professional locksmith or dealer.
notes: XT6 2021-2024. HYQ2ES at 433 MHz (FCC-confirmed 433.92 MHz). Shared with XT4/XT5 2020+. Verify FCC ID on door sticker — some early 2020 units used HYQ2EB (see previous entry).
